current_session.user_request: Implement 8 professional masterclass features using RLM orchestration

task_queue.description: Implement first-class guitar render/cache category, GM guitar routing, procedural fallback, and rock guitar chord-track MIDI routing

result.summary: Added InstrumentCategory.GUITAR with directory/keyword detection so guitar-named strings/funk samples route to guitar while non-guitar strings remain strings. Audio rendering now loads guitar custom melodic cache, maps GM programs 24-31 to guitar, normalizes expansion guitar-family aliases to the guitar cache key, and falls back to a bounded procedural guitar tone. MIDI generation now treats guitar aliases as chord instruments and assigns channel-2 Chords tracks to GM guitar programs for rock/guitar prompts while preserving Rhodes/Ethiopian behavior when guitar is absent.
result.verification: PASS - .\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest tests/test_instrument_manager.py tests/test_audio_renderer.py tests/test_midi_generator.py tests/test_rock_strategy.py tests/test_arranger_rock.py -q passed 17 tests in 2.99s.

task_queue.description: Implement first-class rock-family strategy registration and rock arranger templates with bounded 16-bar smoke behavior

result.summary: Added RockStrategy for rock/classic_rock/alternative_rock/grunge/punk_rock/indie_rock with live-kit GM drums, no trap rolls/claps/808 assumptions, and non-empty root/fifth bass plus power-chord guitar-range chords. Registered rock-family aliases and added rock-family band arrangement templates with a short 16-bar verse/chorus/bridge/chorus path for explicit target_bars smoke runs.
result.verification: PASS - .\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest tests/test_rock_strategy.py tests/test_arranger_rock.py tests/test_strategies.py tests/test_prompt_parser_key.py tests/test_genre_normalization.py -q passed 51 tests in 2.07s; git diff --check passed with no output.

task_queue.description: Implement 1990s rock-family parser keywords, modular genre defaults, instrument-resolution defaults, and focused parser/default regressions

result.summary: Added modular rock-family parser keys (rock, classic_rock, alternative_rock, grunge, punk_rock, indie_rock), band/live-kit defaults, matching normalize_genre aliases and GENRE_DEFAULTS, and instrument-resolution defaults so rock parsing does not fall back to trap_soul/piano-bass. Removed chorus as a choir keyword while preserving chorus as a section/drop hint.
result.verification: PASS - .\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest tests/test_prompt_parser_key.py tests/test_genre_normalization.py -q passed 14 tests in 2.01s.

task_queue.description: Implement 1990s rock smoke harness, organ short-note render fix, and strict audio CI semantics

result.summary: Added a reproducible PowerShell 1990s-rock smoke harness that passes the prompt as one ProcessStartInfo ArgumentList item, writes stdout/stderr logs plus smoke_summary.json, and supports strict --require-audio runs. Clamped organ key-click length and zero-duration output to prevent short-note crashes. Added additive CLI --require-audio semantics so smoke/CI can fail on missing audio or render_status.success=false while preserving default permissive behavior and --require-soundfont semantics.

result.summary: Real smoke exited 0 after polling PID 31368 but parsed the 1990s rock prompt as trap_soul, expanded the intended 16-bar smoke to 28 bars, returned audio null, and captured render_exception diagnostics from generate_organ_tone. The new plan phases parser/defaults/strategy/renderer/analyzer and UI work while preserving existing genres and workflows.

task_queue.description: Disable Python extension terminal auto-activation for this workspace

result.expected_effect: New terminals should stop automatically running .venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1; previously restored terminals may still reflect earlier session state until closed/reopened.
result.verification: get_errors reported no issues in ../.vscode/settings.json.

task_queue.description: Investigate why terminals reopen with the Python .venv activated after VS Code restart

result.finding: No repo-local workspace setting was found that auto-runs Activate.ps1 on terminal open.
result.most_likely_source: VS Code Python extension terminal environment activation and/or terminal session restore.
result.evidence: terminal_last_command returned '& c:\dev\MUSE-ai\MUSE\.venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1', while workspace search found no matching startup hook settings in the repo.

task_queue.description: Apply a safe workspace-only fix to stop stale CMake/cpptools startup churn without altering JUCE project files

result.workspace_fix: Removed the stale cmake.sourceDirectory setting, added cmake.configureOnOpen=false, and replaced dead old-repo C/C++ include paths with current-workspace JUCE source/build paths.
result.safety: No JUCE CMakeLists, package.json build scripts, or runtime files were modified.
result.verification: get_errors reported no issues in ../.vscode/settings.json and ../.vscode/c_cpp_properties.json.

task_queue.description: Investigate the VS Code 'Scan for a kit/compiler' prompt on workspace open and identify whether it originates from repo configuration

result.root_cause: The workspace contains a stale VS Code CMake Tools setting that points cmake.sourceDirectory at an old absolute path outside the current repo, which likely triggers CMake Tools startup and the 'Scan for a kit/compiler' prompt.
secondary_causes: The workspace c_cpp_properties.json also points at removed multimodal-ai-music-gen paths and a hard-coded C:/JUCE/modules path.
The JUCE build folder contains a machine-specific CMake cache with absolute tool paths that can exacerbate reconfigure/toolchain discovery churn.
result.classification: Workspace/extension configuration bug rather than a Python/Node/MUSE runtime bug.
recommended_resolution: Remove or correct ../.vscode/settings.json cmake.sourceDirectory to ${workspaceFolder}/MUSE/juce or delete it if CMake Tools is not needed on workspace open.
Optionally disable CMake Tools for this workspace or set configure-on-open off.
Clean up stale IntelliSense paths in ../.vscode/c_cpp_properties.json and optionally delete MUSE/juce/build local cache.

task_queue.description: Define a pre-implementation three-prompt end-to-end smoke-test plan to run before Milestone 4 Slice 2

plan.scope: A bounded 3-prompt smoke pass was defined to validate prompt-only generation, reference-backed harmonic reuse, and a pre-Slice-2 groove baseline before changing timing transport.
plan.artifacts: The plan uses fixed-seed full generations written to output/_smoke with verbose logging and inspection of generated wav/mid/render-report artifacts.
plan.next_action: Run the three smoke commands, review artifacts, then proceed to Milestone 4 Slice 2 if the baseline and fail-open behavior hold.

task_queue.description: Prepare Milestone 4 Slice 2 as reference groove swing seeding via the existing ParsedPrompt timing path

planned_changes.producer_reuse: Seed a minimal timing.swing_amount field from reference groove data only when the plan does not already define it.
planned_changes.transport_boundary: Carry timing.swing_amount through the score-plan schema and adapter into ParsedPrompt.use_swing / swing_amount using the existing timing path.
planned_changes.bounded_scope: Limit Slice 2 to swing reuse only and explicitly defer timing_humanization, humanization_profile, groove_template, contour reuse, and section-level timing semantics.
planned_changes.proof_plan: Focused JS producer proofs plus adapter transport proofs, including authoritative explicit zero, fail-open handling, and confirmation that the adapted swing value reaches an existing downstream timing sink.
planned_changes.precondition: Run the documented three-prompt smoke pass first so Prompt A/B/C artifacts can establish a pre-Slice-2 baseline for prompt health, harmonic reference reuse, and groove comparison.

task_queue.description: Prepare Milestone 4 Slice 1 as reference chord progression seeding via the existing chord_map path

changes.producer_reuse: ProducerAgent now seeds score-plan chord_map from referenceProfile.raw.chords when a created or refined plan does not already define chord_map and the reference timing/chord data is usable.
changes.bounded_conversion: Reference chord events are converted conservatively from start_time to plan bars using reference BPM/time signature, filtered by confidence and plan range, and collapsed to one deterministic chord per bar.
changes.authority_and_fail_open: Existing chord_map presence remains authoritative and the helper returns plans unchanged for malformed, sparse, low-confidence, or timing-unusable reference data.
changes.proofs: Focused producer reference-chord tests cover seeding, preserve-existing behavior, malformed/sparse fail-open handling, duplicate same-bar collapse, out-of-range dropping, and create/refine wiring.

result.verification: PASS - node copilot-Liku-cli/scripts/test-reference-chord-seeding.js (8 passed, 0 failed); get_errors on producer.js and test-reference-chord-seeding.js returned no errors; post-verifier confirmed the slice remained producer-only and truthful via the existing chord_map runtime path.

task_queue.description: Prepare and implement Milestone 3 Slice 4 as high onset-density drum-light escalation via existing mute_drums repair

changes.analyzer_reuse: generate_corrections() now escalates only explicit too-high onset-density issues in conservative drum-light contexts into the existing mute_drums correction path.
changes.runtime_safety: The new branch is limited to onset_density issues whose parsed expected high bound is <= 2.0, avoiding generic density rewrites and reusing the already-proven downstream mute_drums path.
changes.proofs: Focused analyzer regressions now cover the positive drum-light case, low-density negative case, high-density non-drum-light negative case, and duplicate mute_drums deduplication.
changes.milestone_status: Milestone 3 remains in progress with slices 1-4 complete.

result.verification: PASS - python -m pytest tests/test_output_analyzer.py -q passed (49/49). Final verifier confirmed the slice stayed bounded to onset-density translation, used a conservative current-target drum-light threshold, and preserved mute_drums deduplication.

task_queue.description: Prepare and implement Milestone 3 Slice 3 as analyzer-emitted directional EQ repair plus bounded producer mastering mutation

changes.analyzer_directionality: generate_corrections() now emits explicit too-bright vs too-dark adjust_eq detail for centroid-range mismatches only.
changes.producer_directional_eq: ProducerAgent now treats adjust_eq as actionable only when correction detail is explicitly directional, then applies a small clamped brightness/warmth mastering mutation via existing mastering normalization.
changes.retry_routing: Deterministic structured-repair retry now includes directional adjust_eq only when critic metrics are otherwise green, while non-directional adjust_eq remains guidance-only.
changes.proofs: Focused Python and JS regressions cover high/low centroid directionality, conservative mastering mutation, non-directional no-op EQ, and deterministic retry routing.

changes.runtime_safety: The new register lift applies only when a usable explicit tension arc exists and only when the full shifted section fits the chord instrument range cleanly; missing or unusable arcs remain fail-open at the baseline register.

completed_tasks.description: Analyze broader genre realism, audio-validation best practices, and instrument-frequency awareness after neo-soul hardening

findings.core_gap: The codebase already contains spectral rules, reference matching, sample sonic fingerprints, and register-aware MIDI validation, but those layers are not consistently wired into rendered-audio scoring, repair loops, or genre-wide coverage.
findings.frequency_awareness: Instrument-aware realism is strongest in MIDI note ranges and sample fingerprints, but there is no unified audio-stage role-band or masking validator connecting instrument identity to expected frequency occupancy.
findings.genre_priorities: After neo_soul, the highest-ROI genres to harden are rnb, trap_soul, house, ambient, and Ethiopian variants because they are parseable/generated but can still fall through output-analysis neutral scoring or inconsistent runtime mappings.
findings.implementation_direction: Best next step is to unify genre normalization and reuse existing OutputAnalyzer, GenreRulesEngine, ReferenceAnalyzer, InstrumentAnalyzer, and StylePolicy modules before inventing new analysis systems.

result.verification: PASS - supervisor plus parallel Explore subagents completed a code-grounded read-only audit and converged on the same architecture gaps and rollout priorities.

changes.runtime_normalization: Prompt parsing, microtiming presets, and performance-model mappings now treat neo_soul as a first-class genre instead of falling back through generic rnb handling.

completed_tasks.description: Investigate listened neo-soul quality complaints against generation and validation code paths

findings.validation_gap: ProducerAgent accepts success on MIDI critics alone; rendered-audio analysis is recorded but does not gate success.

findings.rhythm_gap: Rendered-audio analysis uses spectral/percussive heuristics only and does not measure groove pocket, beat stability, or microtiming feel.
findings.generation_gap: Performance-model and microtiming mappings are stronger for rnb than neo_soul, so neo_soul can fall back to generic role defaults in some timing paths.

sprint_9.token_strategy: Supervisor pre-digests all code → edit manifests → merged validator → conditional diagnostician

fixes_applied.BUG-1: Sidechain genre normalization unified with hyphen→underscore replacement
fixes_applied.BUG-2: Sidechain block wrapped in try/except for graceful degradation
fixes_applied.BUG-3: TrackProcessor genre-gated to 11 production genres (classical/jazz/ambient skip it)

fixes_applied.VACUOUS-1: test_track_processor_preset_selection → monkeypatch spy on production mapping

tasks.8.3_reference_profile_bridge: COMPLETE — ReferenceProfile (correct type) created from analysis spectral data, stored in results, forwarded to renderer.set_reference_profile() after renderer instantiation
tasks.8.4_mix_policy_panning: COMPLETE — set_mix_policy() added to AudioRenderer, _compute_pan() extracted as testable method, policy_context.mix forwarded from main.py to renderer

tasks.8.5_drum_pattern_transcription: COMPLETE — kick/snare patterns populated via beat-aligned onset detection in _analyze_drums(), quantized to 16th-note grid, cached librosa.beat.beat_track() for performance
tasks.8.6_musicality_test_expansion: COMPLETE — 6 new musicality tests: trap hihat activity, chord polyphony, scale adherence, bass register, cross-genre velocity, verse-vs-drop density

validation.auditor_stress_tester: recursive-verifier — PASS verdict, 2 MEDIUM findings (triple beat_track call, vacuous fallback test)

fixes_applied.PERF_TRIPLE_BEAT_TRACK: Cached librosa.beat.beat_track() result, reused across 3 call sites (~400ms saved)

tasks.8.7_preset_render_propagation: COMPLETE — set_preset_values() method added to AudioRenderer, _get_preset_target_rms() and _get_preset_reverb_send() extracted as testable helpers, MixPolicy stem_headroom_db and master_ceiling_db consumed in render pipeline, preset_values forwarded from main.py to renderer

validation.auditor: recursive-verifier — CONDITIONAL PASS, 4 MEDIUM (vacuous tests, no wiring test, arithmetic-only MixPolicy tests, optional reverb dependency), 2 LOW
validation.stress_tester: recursive-verifier — 3 BUGs (polished degenerate formula, wrong test ha value, headroom gain cap blocks boosting), 4 FLAWs (ceiling clamp narrow, preset/MixPolicy conflict, comment mismatch, bare except)

fixes_applied.BUG-1: target_rms formula shifted from 0.28-0.12*ha to 0.30-0.14*ha — polished ha=0.25 now gives 0.265 (meaningful bump from 0.25 default)
fixes_applied.BUG-2: Test corrected to use actual polished ha=0.25 (was wrong 0.2), formula mirrors replaced with production code calls to _get_preset_target_rms() and _get_preset_reverb_send()
fixes_applied.BUG-3: Headroom cap changed from 10^(db/20)*2.0 to 10^(-db/20) — at -6dB max gain is 2.0x (was ~1.0x blocking all boosting)
fixes_applied.FLAW-1: master_ceiling_db clamp floor widened from 0.8 to 0.5 — allows -6dB ceiling
fixes_applied.FLAW-2: Interaction comment added documenting preset vs headroom tradeoff
fixes_applied.FLAW-3: Comment range updated to match actual formula range [0.16, 0.30]
